As always, please feel free to say hi if you see me!

Me: “It’s a tech conference called KubeCon.”

Just landed at SLC for Kubecon. Where’s everybody at?

So Kubecon EU is fucking ENORMOUS. I will report on numbers later but first impressions are just that it's simply very big. The @cncf.io is evidently still a critical foundation (pun intended) for modern software workloads.
He just dropped me off and said, “enjoy kubecon” and drove away laughing. ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Things usually go this way: Strong components → system → complexity → components → ... Is the cycle unavoidable?

I wish there were more "outsider guides" to technologies, that explained what it is for people who hear about it or are adjacent to it but don't need to use it. Like what is Kubernetes? What problems does it solve? What makes it a hard problem to solve? What are the legendary warts?

What's inside:

- Crossplane vs Terraform: reconciliation vs one-shot
- Composition Functions: Go code, not YAML patches
- Kyverno guardrails: block $10K RDS instances
- Secrets: MR → XR → Claim → K8s Secret chain
- Debugging: find the real error 3 levels deep

New Podo Stack just dropped.

This week: Crossplane. Turn your K8s cluster into an infrastructure API. Claims, Compositions, Functions, policy guardrails, and debugging.

5 topics that change how your team provisions cloud resources.

Red Hat donating llm-d to CNCF is huge. The gap between 'we have a model' and 'we serve it reliably at scale' is where most AI projects die. Production inference infrastructure is boring and essential - exactly the kind of thing CNCF should own.

It’s official: llm-d has joined the cncf.io ! 🚀

Our mission to evolve Kubernetes into SOTA AI infrastructure just got a massive boost. This milestone belongs to our amazing community. 

Thank you for building this with us. 💜

We’re just getting started!

🔗 www.cncf.io/blog/2026/03...

recent_posts.text: Catching up on #Helm v4 at #kubecon EU… #cloudnative tooling by @robertsirc @sabre1041!

Bluesky's Top 10 Trending Words (past 60min):
💨x169 - 1﹐739
💨x1* - ukraine 🔓
💨x1* - epstein 🔓
💨x1* - democracy 🔓
💨x1* - gaza 🔓
💨x38 - パトレイバー
💨x34 - 2026-03-24
💨x32 - モグコレ
💨x31 - insolvent
💨x27 - 鍛刀
*🔓 = Unlocked Emergency Words (see img)

#FreePalestine 🇵🇸

(Something not right? Reply!)

That was (not surprisingly) a great great talk, with a very concrete and live demo, and a good explanations of all the stakes at hand.

Wholeheartedly recommend anyone interested in the matter, attending #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon or not,  to catch the recording afterward!

Not trying to reinvent Kubernetes built-in authorization though: Kyverno juste creates a SubjectAccessReview, fills it with everything retrieved from the request, and ask Kubernetes whether or not it should be allowed (based on RBAC & co).

Lots of MCP parsing (which Kyverno helps with) in order to check the MCP method, caller tool name, etc.

Unmarshal the resourceKind, apiVersion, resourceGroup, etc., and then this is where the real authorization happens.

Moving to the authorization part, where Kyverno shines.
The dangerous part: the MCP tool called uses the primitves of Kubernetes to essentially just apply YAML. And this can go wild, so you want to control that.

Evaluating with the Envoy mode for the Kyverno ValidationPolicy.

Live demo there too - after quite a few at the keynotes, I love that this live demo spirit was never lost at the #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon

And the demo gods were with the speakers; at least until Wi-Fi was required, but they had a proper backup plan. Props and Kudos to them!

Instead of directly accessing our MCP tool, we'll go through the AgentGateway, and AgentGateway will tell user to use OAuth 2.0 flow to authenticate.

Then, user can talk with kagent-tools. And before the actual tool usage, AgentGateway checks with Kyverno whether the user is allowed to or not.

But each MCP implements its own logic, leading to duplication of authz logic.
Interestingly, that's an issue we've been discussing at @payfiteng.bsky.social over the past couple weeks, so I'm super happy to be at this talk!

This is where #Kyverno and the #AgentGateway come in.

First talk of #KubeCon #CloudNativeCon Day 1 : my dear friend, ex-colleague, and ex-Devoxx-cospeaker @luski.bsky.social from Nirmata ❤️ With @npolshakova.bsky.social from SoloIO, talking about authorizing Agents and MCP tools with AgentGateway and Kyverno ! Would have attended even if not for Luc 🙂

Join Hasith Kalpage at #KubeConEU 2026! AI agents accelerate dev but bring complexity and cost. On March 26th, join the panel to explore proven patterns and insights for successfully deploying AI agents in production. Register here: cs.co/63329B6ZfKW
I only printed 30 pairs of Kubernetes earrings so if you want some, come find me!

We've been having a great few days in Amsterdam already, at Cloud Native Rejekts, Maintainer Summit, and today at the co-located events where colleagues presented several sessions on the @CloudNativePG.bsky.social project. 

Tomorrow, meet us at booth 896 at #KubeCon!

sitting in wait for third now, hopefully it'll be in the goldilocks zone
